description: §19.6.4 + §19.6.6 — the INNERMOST boundary governs E-ERROR-005. The `!`-call sits inside an inner `<errorBoundary>` with NO `fallback`, nested inside an outer boundary that DOES declare a `fallback`. Because §19.6.4 is inner-catches-first, the inner boundary is the one that must display the caught variant; the caught variant (NotFound) has no `renders` and the INNER boundary has no `fallback`, so E-ERROR-005 fires — the outer `fallback` does NOT rescue it (the error never reaches the outer boundary). This is the sharp innermost-governs proof: moving the `fallback` to the INNER boundary (or adding a `renders`) would silence it. Complements the `nested-inner-catches` runtime case (which proves inner-catches-first for a COVERED variant).
rationale: §19.6.4: an inner boundary catches errors before an outer boundary (inner-catches-first). §19.6.6: coverage is checked against the boundary that catches the variant — here the inner boundary, which has no `fallback`, for a variant with no `renders` → E-ERROR-005. The outer `fallback` is irrelevant because the inner boundary intercepts first. E-ERROR-002 stays silent (the call is inside a boundary).
